怯むな！<br>背中を見せたら追撃してくるぞ！: Don't flinch! If you turn your back, they'll chase you down!
はー、これが大穴のモンスターですか。<br>間近でみると、なかなか立派なもので。:
Whoa... So this is a monster from the Abyss. Up close, it's quite
impressive.
余裕があるのはいいが、下手を打つと本当に死ぬぞ。<br>もう少し下がれ。:
It's good that you're confident, but one wrong move and you'll really
die. Move back a bit.
いえいえ～、心配ご無用。<br>……えいっ！: No no, no need to worry. ...Eigh!
――弓を取り出したエメルダは慣れた手つきで矢をつがえ、引き絞る。<br>放たれた:
—Emelda pulled out her bow, nocked an arrow with practiced hands,
and drew it back. The released arrow flew with force and accurately pierced the monster's eye.
どうです？ なかなかのものでしょう？<br>自分の身くらい守れないと、記者なんて:
How about that? Not bad, right? If you can't protect yourself, you
can't be a reporter.
そうだな。……総員！ エメルダの攻撃で片眼が塞がった！<br>死角から回り込んで:
You're right. ...All troops! Emelda's attack took out one eye! Circle
around to its blind spot and wear it down without rushing! Don't push yourselves!
（んっ……？ 特ダネさん、意外と部下想いなんだ？）: (Hm...? Scoop's surprisingly caring for his subordinates?)
（ふぅん……喧嘩ばかりしてた連中だけど、<br>戦闘になるとやっぱり頼りになるの:
(Hmm... They're always quarreling, but when it comes to battle,
they're reliable after all.)
助かったよ、エメルダ。: You were a great help, Emeralda.
いえ。あたしの攻撃がなくても、<br>特ダネさんたちなら問題なく倒せてたでしょ。:
No, even without my attack, Scoop, you and your team could have
taken it down easily, right?
そうかもしれないが、<br>お前のおかげで予定よりも早く大穴の奥地に行けそうだ。:
That may be, but thanks to you, we can get to the deeper parts of the
Abyss ahead of schedule.
まぁ、その前に、お前が驚く姿を見るのが先かもしれないが。:
Well, before that, I might just see that surprised look on your face
first.
大穴の各階層へと短時間で移動するための装置。<br>俺たちはこれを「リフト」と呼:
It's a device for quickly moving between the layers of the Abyss. We
call it the 'Lift'.
俺も詳しくは理解していないが、<br>大穴で見つかる魔石を動力源にしているらしい:
I don't fully understand the details, but apparently it uses the magic
stones found in the Abyss as a power source.
探索が完了した場所を瞬時に移動できるからとても重宝している。:
It's really useful because we can instantly move to any area we've
already explored.
こんなの、都会にだってありませんよ。: There's nothing like this even in the city.
そうだろうな。この世界にはまだ存在しない科学……<br>『オーバーテクノロジー』:
Yeah, I'd imagine so. It's science that doesn't exist in this world yet...
It's one of the Over-Technologies.
ちょっとは前線基地を見直したか？: So, have you changed your opinion of the Frontline Base a little?
まぁ、ちょびっとは。でも合コンとは別腹です。: Well, maybe a little. But the mixer is a different story entirely.
特ダネさんが女の子と過ごしていた酒場じゃないですか！<br>ご飯の前にだなんて、:
Isn't this the tavern where you were with that girl, Scoop? Coming
here before dinner—you're really something...
違う！ 単純に食事をしに来ただけだ！<br>ここの飯はうまいんだぞ？: No! I just came here to eat! The food here's really good, you know?
まぁ、美味しそうではありますよね。<br>ずっと気になってはいました。: Well, it does look delicious. I've been curious about it for a while.
あー！ 戦闘後の酒は格別だ！: Ahh! A drink after a fight really hits the spot!
まったくだ！<br>みんな、飲め飲めーっ！: You said it! Drink up, everyone!
ん？ あの人たち、数日前は喧嘩ばかりしていたのに、<br>けっこう仲良さげじゃな:
Huh? Those guys were always fighting a few days ago, but now they
seem pretty chummy, don't they?
戦場を共に乗り越えると、細かいことは気にしなくなるよ。:
Once you've been through battle together, you stop sweating the
small stuff.
おっ、エメルダ！ 今日はお前のおかげで仕事が楽だった！<br>ありがとな！: Hey, Emeralda! Thanks to you, today's work was a breeze! Thanks!
あ、いえ。どういたしましてー。: Oh, it was nothing. You're welcome.
お前、記者なんだってな？ しかも例の記事を書いたんだろ？<br>次は今日の武勇伝:
So you're a reporter, huh? And you wrote that article, didn't you?
Next time, write a cool story based on today's heroic deeds!
……まぁ、はい。<br>検討しておきます。: ...Well, yes. I'll keep it in mind.
よしよーし！ ってなわけで、飲め飲めーっ！<br>ぎゃははー！: Good, good! So anyway, drink up! Gyahaha!
え？ あたしはソフトドリンクで……: Huh? I'll just stick with a soft drink...
（はぁ……、都会のオシャレなバーでやる合コンとは大違い……）:
(*sigh*... This is a far cry from the group blind dates at trendy city
bars...)
（でも……まぁ、悪い奴らじゃないか。特ダネさんも含めて）: (But... well, I guess they're not bad people. Including Scoop.)
――それから数日間、エメルダは前線基地の皆と行動を共にした。<br>大穴探索で敵:
Over the next several days, Emeralda stayed with everyone at the
Frontline Base. They hunted monsters in the Abyss, shared meals, and chatted idly.
それらの取材結果をもとに、エメルダは記事を書きあげた。: Based on all her research, Emeralda completed an article.
おう、良くやったな。見せてみろ。<br>どれどれ……: Huh, good job. Let me see it. Let's see...
前の記事に比べると、特ダネさんや兵士たちの好感度を上げる内容に<br>なっちゃっ:
Compared to my last, this one ended up painting Scoop and the
soldiers in a positive light, but I think it's a good article!♪
本社復帰は間違いなし。もしかしたら昇進もあるかも！？<br>オホホホーホー♪:
No doubt I'll be back at headquarters. Maybe even a promotion!
Ohohoho!♪
司令官の良いところなんて誰も欲しがってねーよ！<br>前線基地は醜聞以外いらん！:
Nobody's interested in the Commander's good points! The Frontline
Base is only good for scandal!
大事なのは真実でも報道の正確さでもねぇ！ 売り上げだ！<br>嘘ついてでも売れる:
What matters isn't the truth or journalistic accuracy! It's sales!
Rewrite it into something that sells, even if you have to lie! Got it!
ううっ……なんだよぉ、なんなんだよぉ、ちくしょ～……: Ugh... what the hell, what the hell... damn it...
クソ上司めぇ～！ 人の苦労も知らないでぇぇ～<br>文句ばっかりぃぃい～っ！:
That shitty boss! He has no idea what I went through! Always
complaining!
……あの記事で都会に帰れると思ってたのに。<br>戻りたいよぉ……ぐすんっ……:
...I thought that article would get me back to the city. I want to go
back... *sob*...
司令官、こんな夜更けに散歩ですか？<br>あまり羽根を伸ばし過ぎると副司令官に怒:
Commander, taking a walk this late? You'll get chewed out by the
Deputy Commander if you cut loose too much.
まぁ、そう言うな。<br>ちょっとだけだ。ちょっとだけ、な？: Now, don't say that. Just a bit. Just a bit, okay?
（……尾行して特ダネさんの悪いところを全部スッパ抜けば<br>上司に認められて、:
(If I tail Scoop and dig up all his dirty laundry, my boss will recognize
me and I can go back to the city.)
（特ダネさん……悪く思わないでくださいね。<br>あたし、たとえ上司命令でも、や:
(Scoop... don't hold it against me. Even if it's an order from my boss,
I still can't write lies.)
（だから、あなたの真実の姿を――<br>ありのままの醜聞を暴いてみせます――いざ:
(So I'll reveal your true self—the unvarnished scandal—here I come,
it's on!)
